changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Last Night"

TextENGNew: ``......Tomorrow... is already upon us.``
changeReason: The original omits the nuance of 'もう' (already) present in the Japanese; the new translation conveys the imminence that time has run out.

TextENGNew: ``Hm? ......Oh, you mean the anniversary of our deaths?``
changeReason: The Japanese '命日' specifically means a death anniversary (a recurring memorial date), not the act of dying; the new translation captures this distinction.

TextENGNew: ``......This time will end the same way as always.``
changeReason: 'As always' implies a long-repeated pattern matching the context of Rika's looping existence, while 'just like the last time' narrows it to a single prior instance.

TextENGNew: ``I wonder about that. ...It has been very similar, but there were some differences this time around.@k@vS20/05/440500050. ...Keiichi remembered what happened before.``
``...That is impossible. There is no way he could remember--no, there is no way he could *know*.``
``Hee hee hee... The more adamantly you insist it is absolutely impossible... the more it makes me feel that this truly is a miracle.``
``It is pointless. This time will end the same way again.``
``Will it, I wonder?``
``It will all be over soon. Everything will end.@k@vS20/12/441100011. ...Yes, when the higurashi cry.``
changeReason: The original omits 'もうすぐ' (soon) and restructures the three-part statement into two, losing the emphatic pacing and the iconic title reference present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ...Was it all going to end regardless?
The more you roll a die, the closer the cumulative total approaches the statistical average.
Even if you roll it a hundred times and a miracle occurs--sixes appearing in a row--the grand total still converges on the mean.
...Subsumed into the predetermined harmony of probability.
changeReason: The original reduces '予定調和に内包されてしまう' (subsumed into predetermined harmony) to a generic 'fact of statistics,' losing the philosophical metaphor central to Rika's worldview.

TextENGNew: And yet, if you roll a hundred times, the range of possible totals spans from 100 to 600.
The more you roll, the more the future tries to collapse into a single average--and yet,@k the more you roll, the more possible futures actually come into existence.
changeReason: The original uses 'outcomes' where the Japanese specifically says '未来' (futures), losing the thematic connection between dice rolls and possible futures central to this passage's metaphor.

TextENGNew: A hundred rolls yields roughly five hundred possible futures.
A thousand rolls yields five thousand possible futures.
Surely among them, there must be one outcome where this never-ending board game finally reaches its conclusion.
changeReason: The Japanese says 'スゴロク' (sugoroku, a board game), not a dice game; the new translation correctly identifies it as a board game.

TextENGNew: ...The miracle Keiichi showed me was a revelation--proof that continuing to roll the die is never futile.
Do not surrender to the tyranny of averages.
``............What an infuriating back you show me. ...You are absolutely convinced this time will fail too. That melancholy of yours is written all over you.``
changeReason: The original changes '背中' (back) to 'face' and omits '哀愁' (melancholy) and 'いらつく' (infuriating); the new preserves Rika's observation about the other character's dejected back.

TextENGNew: ``...Even I... think it will probably end the same way again... But I refuse to give up until the very last die is cast.@k@vS20/05/440500055. Keiichi taught me that.@k@vS20/05/440500056. Something is different this time.@k@vS20/05/440500057. ...So I will try believing in that.``
``......Rika... you truly are strong.``
``Well, of course. I am much younger than you, after all.``
``......Mmm... mumble mumble...... ...Rika...?``
changeReason: 'むにゃむにゃ' is sleepy mumbling, not a yawn; the new translation correctly renders the onomatopoeia.
'みー' is Rika's signature catchphrase pronounced 'Mii'; 'Meep' is a different word that could give readers a different impression of her character voice.

TextENGNew: ``Why are you still up at this hour...? ...Yaaawn... Staying up late is bad for you, you know...``
``...I am sorry. I could not sleep, so I was just looking at the stars.@k@vS20/05/440500061. I will go to bed now.``
``Please do...... Good night, Rika......``
``...Good night, Satoko.``

changeReason: Original translation was empty; new translation provides content.

TextENGNew: She still does not understand.
changeReason: Original translation was empty; new translation provides content.

TextENGNew: This child still... has not come to 'understand'......
changeReason: Original translation was empty; new translation provides content.
